MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 28, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 13, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-28-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION, PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY HEARING SET FOR MAY-13-2014 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF PLEADS AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY AND DECLARATORY RELIEF IS APPROPRIATE. EVEN IF THE COURT AGREED WITH DEFENDANT'S INTERPRETATION OF THE RECYCLING ACT, IT WOULD BE IMPROPER TO SUSTAIN DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER. "IN AN ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF, WHEN THE COMPLAINT SHOWS THE EXISTENCE OF AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY AMONG THE PARTIES, A GENERAL DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT SHOULD BE OVERRULED. THE PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO A DECLARATION OF HIS RIGHTS WHETHER THE DECLARATION IS FAVORABLE TO HIM OR NOT.'" GENERAL INS. CO. OF AMERICA V. WHITMORE (1965) 235 CAL.APP.2D 670, 673; SEE ALSO HAWKINS V. MCLAUGHLIN (1961) 196 CAL.APP.2D 318, 329 ("AS HERETOFORE STATED, THE COURT SHOULD NOT DISPOSE OF THE CASE ON DEMURRER MERELY BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO A FAVORABLE DECLARATION. * * * 'TO HOLD THAT A PLAINTIFF ON THE WRONG SIDE OF A CONTROVERSY IS NOT ENTITLED TO THE SECURITY AND RELIEF AGAINST UNCERTAINTY WHICH A DECLARATORY JUDGMENT AFFORDS WOULD REQUIRE US TO READ INTO THE STATUTE A LIMITATION NOT THERE PRESENT. * * *'") (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar May-28-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is overruled. Plaintiff pleads an actual controversy and declaratory relief is appropriate. Even if the court agreed with defendant's interpretation of the Recycling Act, it would be improper to sustain defendant's demurrer. "In an action for declaratory relief, when the complaint shows the existence of an actual controversy among the parties, a general demurrer to the complaint should be overruled. The plaintiff is entitled to a declaration of his rights whether the declaration is favorable to him or not.'" General Ins. Co. of America v. Whitmore (1965) 235 Cal.App.2d 670, 673; see also Hawkins v. McLaughlin (1961) 196 Cal.App.2d 318, 329 ("As heretofore stated, the court should not dispose of the case on demurrer merely because the plaintiff is not entitled to a favorable declaration. * * * 'To hold that a plaintiff on the wrong side of a controversy is not entitled to the security and relief against uncertainty which a declaratory judgment affords would require us to read into the statute a limitation not there present. * * *'") (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Continued To May-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF RESOURCES RECYCLING AND RECOVERY'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is continued to May 28, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 13, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
